creator: msoulier@digitaltorque.ca
text:
On Mon, Sep 22, 2008 at 11:41 AM, Remko Lodder <remko@freebsd.org> wrote:
> Michael P. Soulier wrote:
>
> Do you have something special in /etc/make.conf ? Perhaps the mirror you
> choose was not up to speed with the correct information? Please retry using
> a different mirror to see whether it works, also do not forget
> to nuke /usr/obj so that you have a really fresh build.
>
> I -never- ran into this while doing various upgrades lately.

I found out about 30 minutes ago that it was my own bloody fault. :)

I was using the ports version of openssh and only recently switched
back to the base version, but I forgot to update make.conf. This is
not a bug, although the output of make from libssh might have been a
tad more informative.

Thanks,
Mike
-- 
Michael P. Soulier <msoulier@digitaltorque.ca>
"Any intelligent fool can make things bigger and more complex... It takes a
touch of genius - and a lot of courage to move in the opposite direction."
--Albert Einstein
